title: veronica orchidea var. eglandulosa
intro: Veronica orchidea var. eglandulosa, also known as the "veronica orchidea", is a species of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, Veronica orchidea var. eglandulosa is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be applied topically as an ointment or cream for its wound-healing properties.
